headline: The AI Certification That Comes With A Job — Not Just A Diploma
mechanism: Deploy-First Methodology: You build real AI systems for real companies during training, so you graduate with a portfolio AND a placement — not just theory
lead: Right now, 73% of businesses say they need AI integration help but can't find qualified people. You've seen the job postings. You've watched the market explode. You know AI is the opportunity of a decade. But here's what nobody tells you about AI training programs: most of them teach you tools that are outdated by graduation day. AI Integrators is different because we don't teach you AI in a vacuum — we embed you in live business deployments from Week 1.
body: Dan Kennedy would tell you: 'The best education is the one that pays you back before you finish paying for it.' That's exactly what we built. While other programs have you watching pre-recorded videos about ChatGPT prompts, our 12-week AI Automation Certification puts you in the trenches deploying real AI systems for real companies. Week 1-4: You master the core AI automation stack — not surface-level theory, but the actual deployment frameworks companies pay $15K-$50K to implement. Week 5-8: You're assigned to a live business project. You build, you deploy, you iterate. This isn't a simulation. The business is real. The problems are real. The solutions you create are real. Week 9-12: Our placement team activates. They've been building relationships with companies who need AI integrators since before you enrolled. By graduation, you don't send resumes into a black hole. You walk into interviews with a portfolio of deployed systems and a certification that means 'this person has actually done the work.' The mechanism is simple: we created the training program that companies told us they wished existed. Then we pre-sold those same companies on hiring our graduates.
offer_stack:
HERE'S EVERYTHING YOU GET:
- Complete 12-Week AI Automation Certification ($9,997 value)
- Live Business Deployment Projects — not simulations ($5,000 value)
- Guaranteed Job Placement Program ($3,000 value)
- Private AI Integrators Community — lifetime access ($2,997/yr value)
- Weekly Live Q&A with working AI consultants ($1,997 value)
- AI Tools & Templates Library — updated monthly ($997 value)
TOTAL VALUE: $23,988
YOUR INVESTMENT: $4,997
That's less than a single month's salary in the role you'll land.
cta: Enrollment closes Friday at midnight. We only accept 30 students per cohort because each one gets placed in a live deployment project — and we only have 30 active business partners this quarter. Once seats fill, the next cohort doesn't start for 12 weeks. Apply now. Your interview is a 15-minute call to make sure this is right for you. If it's not, we'll tell you.

headline: Warning: Don't Learn AI Until You Read This
mechanism: Reverse-Engineered Curriculum: Built by interviewing 200+ companies about what they actually need, then designing training around those exact deployment gaps
lead: Every week another AI bootcamp launches. Every week another batch of graduates discovers their 'certification' is worth about as much as the PDF it's printed on. The problem isn't that these programs teach bad information. The problem is they teach the WRONG information — skills that sound impressive on a syllabus but that no hiring manager has ever asked for.
body: We spent 6 months interviewing CTOs, operations directors, and business owners before we wrote a single lesson. We asked them one question: 'If you could hire an AI integrator tomorrow, what would they need to know how to do?' Their answers had nothing to do with building models from scratch or writing papers about transformer architecture. They wanted people who could: Deploy AI automations that save 20+ hours per week in specific departments. Connect AI tools to existing business systems without breaking everything. Manage AI projects from scoping to deployment without hand-holding. That became our curriculum. Not what sounds impressive — what gets hired. Our 12-week program is structured around the 14 most-requested AI deployment scenarios in small and mid-market businesses. You learn each one by doing it — on a live project with a real company. When you graduate, you don't need to 'break into AI.' You're already in it.
offer_stack:
THE AI INTEGRATORS PACKAGE:

- Job Placement Guarantee — we place you or you don't pay ($3,000 value)

TODAY: $4,997

No questions. No hoops. We can make this guarantee because we've already pre-sold companies on our graduates. Apply today — 30 seats per cohort. Current cohort is 60% filled.

headline: Companies Are Paying $150K/Year For AI Integrators. Here's How To Become One In 12 Weeks.
mechanism: Placement Pipeline: A closed-loop system where the companies funding the program are the same ones hiring graduates — creating a self-sustaining employment engine
lead: The AI gold rush isn't about building the next ChatGPT. It's about being the person who walks into an overwhelmed business and says, 'I'll deploy AI across your operations in 30 days.' That person — the AI Integrator — is the most in-demand role in business right now. And almost nobody is training for it correctly.
body: Here's why most AI training fails: they teach you AI. We teach you AI integration. The difference? An AI student knows how GPT works. An AI Integrator knows how to walk into a 50-person company, audit their operations, identify the 5 highest-ROI automation opportunities, deploy them, and train the team to use them — all in 30 days. That's the skill set companies are paying $120K-$180K per year for. And there aren't enough people who can do it. Our 12-week certification was designed in partnership with the companies doing the hiring. We asked them: What do you need? They told us. We built it. Then we said: 'Great — now hire our graduates.' They agreed. That's the Placement Pipeline. It's not a job board. It's not 'career support.' It's a direct line from certification to employment, because the employers helped design the certification.

headline: The 12-Week Shortcut To A $150K AI Career (No Coding Required)
mechanism: Zero-to-Deployed Framework: A modular training system that takes complete beginners through 14 AI deployment scenarios in 12 weeks, each one building on the last
lead: You don't need to be a programmer. You don't need a computer science degree. You don't even need to know what an API is — yet. What you need is the willingness to spend 12 weeks learning the most valuable skill set in the modern economy: deploying AI systems that save businesses time and money.
body: Most AI programs are built by engineers for engineers. Ours was built by deployment specialists for deployment specialists. There's a massive difference. Engineers build AI tools. Deployment specialists take those tools and wire them into businesses. The first requires a PhD. The second requires our 12-week program. Here's the Zero-to-Deployed Framework: Weeks 1-3: Foundation. You learn the AI automation landscape, the major tools, and how businesses actually use them. No theory for theory's sake — every concept is tied to a deployment scenario. Weeks 4-7: Build. You work through 7 of our 14 core deployment scenarios — each one modeled on a real engagement that generated $10K-$50K in client value. Weeks 8-10: Deploy. You're matched with a live business project. You scope it, build it, deploy it, and document it. This becomes your portfolio piece. Weeks 11-12: Place. Our placement team connects you with companies actively hiring AI integrators. You interview with your portfolio in hand — proof that you can do the job, not just talk about it.

headline: Why Smart People Are Leaving Tech Jobs To Become AI Integrators
mechanism: Career Arbitrage Model: Exploiting the gap between massive corporate AI budgets and the tiny pool of people who can actually deploy AI systems — positioning graduates in the highest-demand, lowest-supply role in tech
lead: Something strange is happening in tech. Senior developers at Google, product managers at Salesforce, and consultants at McKinsey are quietly leaving their six-figure jobs to become AI Integrators. Not because they're taking a pay cut — because they're getting a raise. And not because the work is easier — because the demand is insane.
body: There's a gap in the market that most people can't see yet. Companies have allocated billions for AI. They've bought the tools. They've signed the contracts. But they can't find people to actually deploy the systems. It's like having a warehouse full of rockets and no one who knows how to launch them. That's the AI Integrator opportunity. And it's not going to last forever. Right now, there are roughly 10 qualified AI integrators for every 100 companies that need one. That imbalance is why salaries are $120K-$180K. That's why our graduates are getting multiple offers. That's why this program exists. We saw the gap. We talked to the companies. We built the training they said they needed. Then we built the pipeline so our graduates walk straight into those roles. 12 weeks. That's the time between today and your first day as a paid AI Integrator.

headline: From Zero To $127K: The 12-Week AI Career That Nobody Sees Coming
mechanism: The Invisible Career Path: While everyone fights over AI engineering roles, AI Integrators quietly fill the highest-demand, lowest-competition position in tech
lead: While a million people learn to code AI, almost nobody is learning to deploy it. That's the gap. Companies don't need more people who can build language models. They need people who can walk in Monday morning and have AI running by Friday. That person — the AI Integrator — starts at $127K on average and there aren't enough of them. By a factor of 10.
body: Think about what happened with the internet. The people who got rich weren't the ones who built browsers. They were the ones who helped businesses USE the internet — web designers, digital marketers, e-commerce consultants. AI is following the same pattern. And right now, we're in the 'businesses know they need it but can't find anyone to do it' phase. That's where AI Integrators come in. Our 12-week certification trains you for the role companies are desperately trying to fill. Not AI research. Not machine learning engineering. AI Integration — the practical, deployment-focused work of connecting AI tools to business operations. Our graduates come from every background: teachers, accountants, project managers, restaurant owners. They don't need coding skills. They need our training, our deployment practice, and our placement pipeline. The results from our first 6 cohorts: 94% placement rate within 60 days. Average starting offer: $127,000. 73% placed by companies they worked with during the program. Average age of graduate: 34. Most common previous career: non-tech professional.

lead: Let's be honest: most AI certifications aren't worth the credit card debt they create. You know it. We know it. The market knows it. So here's what we did differently: we built the guarantee FIRST. Before we designed a single lesson, we secured partnerships with 47 companies and asked: 'If we train people to YOUR specs, will you hire them?' They said yes. Then we built the training.

That's why we can make this promise: complete the program, do the work, and we'll place you in a paid AI integration role within 90 days. Full refund if we don't. lead: Not a projection. Not a 'potential' salary. $127,000 is the verified average starting offer across 159 graduates from 6 completed cohorts. And the number is trending up — Cohort 6 averaged $138,000. The reason is simple economics: demand for AI integrators is growing faster than supply. We're one of the only programs producing deployment-ready AI professionals. And companies pay a premium for people who can actually do the work.
body: I could tell you about our curriculum, our methodology, and our teaching philosophy. But you're Solution Aware — you know AI training exists, you know it matters, and you're comparing options. So let me give you what you actually need to make this decision: THE MECHANISM: We partner with companies, they design the curriculum, you train on their real problems, they hire you. It's a pipeline, not a program. THE PROOF: 169 graduates. 159 placed. 94.1% rate. $127K average. THE SPEED: 12 weeks to certification. Average 47 days from graduation to first paycheck. THE RISK: Zero. 90-day placement guarantee with full refund. THE COST: $4,997. That's 3.9% of what you'll earn in year one. WHAT YOU'LL DO: Deploy real AI systems for real companies during the program. Walk out with a portfolio, a certification, and — in most cases — a job offer from the company you worked with. WHAT YOU WON'T DO: Watch pre-recorded lectures. Complete theoretical assignments. Build toy projects nobody cares about. Wonder whether you'll find a job.

headline: I Asked 47 CTOs: 'What Does Your Dream AI Hire Look Like?' Then I Built The Training Program They Described.
mechanism: CTO-reverse-engineered training — first-person founder narrative
lead: Three years ago, I couldn't find AI integrators for my own company. Nobody could. I'd interview people with impressive AI certifications who couldn't deploy a single automation in a live business. So I called 47 CTOs and asked: 'What do you actually need?' Their answers were shockingly consistent. And shockingly different from what every AI program teaches.
body: Every CTO said the same thing in different words: 'I don't need someone who understands neural networks. I need someone who can walk into my business on Monday and have AI saving us time by Friday.' So I built that training. 12 weeks. 14 deployment scenarios based on the exact situations these CTOs face. Live projects with real companies — not simulations. And a direct pipeline to employment because the companies who defined the training are the same ones doing the hiring. The results speak: 94% placement rate. $127K average starting salary. 73% hired by their practicum company. 0 refunds requested under our placement guarantee. But here's the thing that surprises people most: the average graduate isn't a tech person. They're project managers, teachers, office administrators, small business owners. People who know how to get things done but just didn't have the AI deployment skills. Until now. Jennifer Walsh was an executive assistant making $52K. She deployed an AI meeting and task management system during her practicum. The company hired her at $115K to expand it across all their departments. 12 weeks. 121% salary increase. That's not a marketing claim — that's Jennifer's story, verified and documented.

headline: We Don't Teach AI. We Deploy You Into Companies That Need It. (94% Placement Rate.)
mechanism: Deployment-first immersion — you don't learn then apply, you learn BY deploying
lead: Imagine starting a new career where Day 1 isn't a lecture — it's a deployment briefing. Where your 'homework' is building AI systems for real companies. Where your 'final exam' is a job offer. That's AI Integrators. We don't teach AI in a classroom and hope you figure out how to use it. We deploy you into companies that need AI and teach you while you do the work.
body: This model came from frustration. Traditional model: Learn → Practice → Graduate → Search → Interview → Maybe get hired (6-18 months). Our model: Learn by deploying → Build portfolio → Graduate → Get hired by the company you just worked with (12 weeks + 47 days average). The difference isn't marginal. It's structural. When you learn by deploying, three things happen simultaneously: 1) You acquire practical skills that can't be taught in a classroom. 2) You build a portfolio of real work with measurable results. 3) You form a working relationship with a company that already knows your capabilities. That's why 73% of our graduates are hired by their practicum company. They don't need to prove they can do the job — the company already watched them do it. Data across 6 cohorts: 169 graduates, 159 placed, average salary $127K, average time to placement 47 days. The remaining 6% (10 graduates) chose to go independent as AI consultants and are averaging $150K+ in their first year. Nobody has requested a refund under our placement guarantee.

headline: 47 Companies Are Waiting To Hire You. You Just Need 12 Weeks Of Training They Designed.
mechanism: Company-commissioned pipeline — employer-designed curriculum leading directly to placement
lead: This is not a metaphor. Right now, 47 companies have signed agreements to interview and hire graduates of our AI Integrators program. These are the same companies that designed our curriculum — they told us what to teach, we taught it, and they hire the people we train. 94% placement rate. $127,000 average starting salary. 12 weeks from enrollment to certification.
body: Let me show you why every other AI program is playing a different game. Other programs: Built by instructors → Based on what's 'interesting' → Graduates enter open job market → Compete with thousands → Average 4.7 months to find a job → Average starting salary $78K. AI Integrators: Built by employers → Based on what they actually need → Graduates enter closed pipeline → Compete with nobody → Average 47 days to placement → Average starting salary $127K. The difference is the pipeline. We didn't build a school and then try to find jobs for graduates. We found 47 companies that couldn't hire AI integrators, asked them what they needed, built the training around their exact requirements, and connected the two ends. Six cohorts. 169 graduates. 159 placed. Here are the types of people who succeed in this program: Non-tech professionals who want a career change (52% of graduates). Tech workers who want to specialize in AI deployment (31%). Business owners who want to add AI services (12%). Career changers from any background (5%). Ages range from 24 to 58. The most common previous job title among our graduates is 'project manager.' The second most common is 'teacher.' This is not a program for engineers. It's a program for people who get things done.

headline: The AI Career Nobody's Talking About Pays $127K And Takes 12 Weeks To Start. Here's The Door.
mechanism: Hidden career path + employer pipeline
lead: While everyone argues about whether AI will take jobs, a quiet group of people are walking into new careers that didn't exist two years ago — and earning $127,000 to start. They're called AI Integrators. They don't build AI. They deploy it. And companies are so desperate to hire them that 47 of them partnered with us to train the next batch.
body: You've probably never heard of an AI Integrator. That's the point. This role lives in the gap between 'AI tools exist' and 'businesses actually using them.' Engineers build the tools. AI Integrators deploy them. Think of it as the difference between designing a car and being the mechanic who keeps the fleet running. One requires a degree and years of study. The other requires practical training and the willingness to learn — and right now, it pays almost as much. Here's why: Every mid-market business in America is sitting on AI tools they've purchased but can't implement. They need someone who can walk in, assess their operations, identify AI opportunities, and deploy solutions that save time and money. That person makes $127K on average. There aren't enough of them. So 47 companies came to us and said: build us more. We did. Our 12-week AI Integrators certification has now produced 169 graduates. 159 are placed in paid roles. The other 10 went independent and earn even more. Nobody asked for a refund. Every cohort has a waitlist. The mechanism is simple: companies design our training → you complete the training → those same companies hire you. It works because we eliminated the gap between 'what's taught' and 'what's needed.'

headline: Stop 'Learning AI.' Start Deploying It. (47 Companies Will Pay You $127K To Do Exactly That.)
mechanism: Deploy-don't-learn reframe with employer pipeline
lead: Every week, 10,000 people sign up for another AI course. They watch videos. They earn badges. They update LinkedIn. And 4.7 months later, most of them still don't have an AI job. We stopped making courses. We started making AI Integrators — people who deploy AI systems in live businesses and get paid $127K to do it. The difference? Our graduates don't 'learn' AI. They deploy it. In real companies. Starting in Week 5.
body: Here's the uncomfortable truth about the AI education market: it's built to sell courses, not to create employable people. There are 400+ AI programs, bootcamps, and certifications available right now. Their combined placement rate is estimated at 23%. Ours is 94%. Why? Because we built the program backwards. We started with the end result — a hired AI Integrator — and worked backward to figure out what training that required. The answer came from 47 companies who told us: 'Teach them to deploy AI customer service systems. Teach them to build AI workflow automations. Teach them to create AI reporting dashboards. Teach them to scope projects and manage implementations. Don't teach them to build models or write research papers. We need operators, not academics.' So that's what we teach. 14 deployment scenarios, practiced live on real company projects. 12 weeks. Then placement. It's worked 159 out of 169 times. Cohort-by-cohort average salary trend: $118K → $122K → $131K → $129K → $134K → $138K. The market is getting more desperate for AI integrators, not less. And salaries reflect it.

headline: Your Next Certification Won't Get You Hired. Ours Will. (Or You Pay Nothing.)
mechanism: Guarantee-first positioning with competitive destruction
lead: We tracked 12 popular AI certification programs. Their average time-to-employment for graduates: 4.7 months. Their average starting salary: $78,000. Their placement rate: 23%. Now here's us: time-to-employment: 47 days. Starting salary: $127,000. Placement rate: 94%. And if we don't place you? Full refund. No one else makes that guarantee because no one else has this pipeline.
body: The AI certification market is broken in a specific way: programs are designed to maximize enrollment, not employment. Big class sizes. Pre-recorded content. No employer relationships. Generic curriculum. The result? Thousands of 'certified' people fighting for the same entry-level roles, armed with badges that mean nothing to hiring managers. We fixed every piece of that system: Class size: 30. Not 300, not 3,000. Thirty. Content: Live, deployment-focused, updated every cohort based on employer feedback. Employer relationships: 47 companies. They co-designed the curriculum. They provide practicum projects. They hire graduates. Curriculum: 14 deployment scenarios that directly match what employers need done. Placement: Dedicated specialist for each graduate. 73% hired by practicum company. 94% placed within 60 days. This is what happens when you build a program around outcomes instead of enrollment numbers. It doesn't scale to 10,000 students. It's not supposed to. It scales to 30 graduates per cohort who walk out with $127K jobs. The data across 6 cohorts and 169 graduates is public and verified.
